timeline.label: Q2 FY2026: revenue $18.0B (+8% USD); bookings $22.1B (+1% LC); EPS $2.93 — early bookings stabilization after FY2025 flat bookings
FY2025 results: revenue $69.7B (+7% LC); bookings $80.6B (−1% LC); OCF $11.47B; $8.3B returned to shareholders
Business optimization initiated Q4 FY2025: $615M charges for headcount reductions; adjusted operating margin 15.6%
Board adopted Julie Sweet chair/CEO compensation framework for FY2026 proxy cycle
Geographic reorganization: Latin America moved to Americas; Growth Markets renamed Asia Pacific (comparables restated)
Announced $3B multi-year generative AI investment to build reinvention capabilities and capture enterprise AI spend
Completed prior business optimization program ($438M FY2024 charges); FY2024 adjusted EPS $11.95
Julie Sweet became chair of the board (added to CEO role held since 2019)
Julie Sweet appointed chief executive officer, succeeding Pierre Nanterme
Accenture plc incorporated in Ireland; NYSE listing as ACN following 2001 IPO structure

industry.structure: Global IT and consulting services oligopoly: Accenture, IBM Consulting, Indian IT majors (TCS, Infosys), and Big Four advisory compete on scale, price, and specialization. Switching costs exist on multi-year transformations but not monopoly pricing.
industry.share_shift: Accenture maintains revenue leadership; managed services growing faster than consulting (9% vs 6% FY2025). Indian firms gain on cost; hyperscaler in-house services compete on cloud-native work.
industry.trend: Generative AI is simultaneously a demand catalyst (enterprise reinvention) and a supply threat (fewer billable hours per project). Clients shifting spend toward recurring managed services and AI-enabled operations.
